data.title: Fortinet And D&H Partnership Aims To Deepen Channel Security Adoption
data.description:
• Fortinet (NasdaqGS:FTNT) has partnered with D&H Distributing to integrate its full security portfolio into the D&H Advanced Solutions+ ecosystem.
  • The companies are launching an exclusive enablement program to help channel partners build skills around Fortinet’s security technologies.
  • The collaboration targets VARs, MSPs, and enterprise IT providers in the U.S. channel market.

For investors watching NasdaqGS:FTNT, this move sits squarely in Fortinet’s core business of network and enterprise cybersecurity. As more IT spending flows through distributors and service providers, vendors that can plug into these ecosystems and offer hands-on support often gain more visibility with end customers.

The new enablement program may matter if you follow how Fortinet positions itself against other security vendors in the enterprise space. By giving partners access to training and technical resources inside D&H’s platform, Fortinet is aiming to deepen product adoption and potentially capture a larger share of future security deployments through the channel.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Fortinet by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Fortinet.

How Fortinet stacks up against its biggest competitors

For Fortinet, being woven into D&H’s Advanced Solutions+ ecosystem puts its full product set in front of a broad mix of VARs, MSPs, MSSPs and enterprise providers that already rely on D&H for security offerings. The FWD enablement program, with structured NSE aligned training, hands on labs and go to market toolkits, is designed to position Fortinet as the default choice when those partners design and manage client security projects.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Fortinet Narrative

Some investors view Fortinet through the lens of how effectively it can work with large distributors and service providers, and this agreement speaks directly to that narrative. With analysts currently split between Hold ratings and at least one Buy rating, this partnership is one of the developments investors may watch to see how Fortinet executes on broader channel centric plans.

Fortinet: Key Rewards And Watchpoints From This Deal

  • Broader access to D&H’s U.S. partner base may help Fortinet reach more mid market and enterprise customers through trusted intermediaries.
  • The FWD program’s focus on certifications, workshops and cross portfolio integration can encourage deeper use of Fortinet’s stack within partner offerings.
  • Success depends on partners actually committing time to training and transitioning their existing security practices to Fortinet technologies.
  • Fortinet still competes with other security vendors inside distributor channels, so share of wallet across partners is not guaranteed.

What To Watch Next

From here, it is worth watching how quickly D&H partners adopt Fortinet training, whether Fortinet features more visibly in managed security bundles, and how management references channel traction in future updates. To keep up with how investors and analysts interpret developments like this, you can follow ongoing commentary through community narratives on Fortinet.

data.title: Assessing Marathon Digital Holdings (MARA) Valuation After Tariff Shock And Bitcoin Driven Volatility
data.description:
Advertisement

Tariff shock puts MARA’s Bitcoin exposure and short interest in focus

Recent US tariffs on several European countries, along with the escalation of trade tensions, hit Bitcoin-related equities. MARA Holdings (MARA) saw volatility as one of the largest Bitcoin treasury holders.

See our latest analysis for MARA Holdings.

At a share price of $10.56, MARA has seen a 1 day share price return of 1.83% and a 30 day share price return of 4.24%. However, this sits against a 90 day share price return decline of 45.06% and a 1 year total shareholder return decline of 46.37%. Recent momentum has softened after earlier gains, although the 3 year total shareholder return of 20.69% points to a mixed longer term picture.

If tariff headlines and Bitcoin volatility have you looking beyond a single stock, this could be a good moment to scan fast growing stocks with high insider ownership for other ideas catching investors’ attention.

With the share price well below analyst targets and short sellers heavily involved, the market clearly has strong views on MARA. The key question is whether this recent weakness signals a potential opportunity or if expectations for future growth are already fully reflected.

Price-to-Earnings of 4.3x: Is it justified?

At a last close of $10.56, MARA trades on a P/E of 4.3x, which screens as low compared to both peers and the wider US market.

P/E compares the share price with earnings per share, so a lower figure can suggest the market is attaching a relatively modest price to each dollar of profit. For a Bitcoin exposed, software classified business that has moved into profitability, this kind of earnings multiple is often where investors look first.

Several checks point to the market pricing MARA below peers. The shares are described as trading at good value compared to peers and the US Software industry, and the current P/E of 4.3x sits well below the peer average of 26.6x and the industry average of 30.1x. Against an estimated fair P/E of 6.3x, the current ratio also sits at a discount, which suggests some room for the multiple to move closer to that level if conditions line up with expectations.

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 4.3x (UNDERVALUED)

However, you are still facing clear risks, including Bitcoin price swings feeding into earnings volatility and a recent net income growth decline of 98%, which could pressure sentiment.

data.title: Is Comcast’s New Xfinity Membership Push Reframing Its Customer Strategy for CMCSA Investors?
data.description:
• Comcast has recently launched Xfinity Membership, a new loyalty experience that bundles weekly perks and exclusive rewards across its brands, while continuing to expand its physical footprint with new Xfinity Stores in South DeKalb, Georgia and Chehalis, Washington.
  • Together, the enhanced membership program and interactive retail locations highlight Comcast’s push to deepen customer relationships by tying its connectivity, media, and mobile offerings into a more integrated ecosystem.
  • Next, we’ll examine how this push to integrate Xfinity Membership with expanded retail access shapes Comcast’s broader investment narrative for investors.

We've found 13 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

What Is Comcast's Investment Narrative?

For Comcast, the core investment belief is that its broadband, media, and wireless assets can be stitched into a stickier ecosystem, even as growth expectations stay muted and earnings are forecast to contract. Xfinity Membership and the new South DeKalb and Chehalis stores fit that story as tactical moves to deepen engagement, but they are unlikely to shift the main near term catalysts on their own. Those still center on how Comcast manages slowing domestic broadband momentum, maintains its high dividend, and balances debt with continued investment across cable, streaming, and theme parks. The recent stock underperformance and low earnings multiple suggest the market is already pricing in many of these concerns, so loyalty and retail initiatives look more like incremental support than a fundamental reset of the risk profile.

However, Comcast’s high debt load remains a key issue investors should not ignore. Comcast's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Ten fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly US$28 to just over US$101 per share, showing how far apart individual views can be. Set against Comcast’s earnings decline forecasts and high leverage, this spread underlines why it helps to weigh multiple perspectives before deciding how these risks and catalysts might play out.

data.title: Should IDEXX's New AI Veterinary Diagnostics Push Require Action From IDEXX Laboratories (IDXX) Investors?
data.description:
• In January 2026, IDEXX Laboratories announced new veterinary diagnostics offerings, including the ImageVue DR50 Plus digital imaging system and expanded IDEXX Cancer Dx Panel capabilities for canine mast cell tumor detection, alongside FNA cytology on the inVue Dx analyzer.
  • Together, these launches deepen IDEXX’s integration of AI and connected workflows in veterinary practices, aiming to speed earlier cancer detection and improve imaging efficiency while emphasizing radiation safety.
  • We’ll now examine how IDEXX’s push into AI-powered, end-to-end veterinary diagnostics workflows shapes the company’s broader investment narrative.

The end of cancer? These 29 emerging AI stocks are developing tech that will allow early identification of life changing diseases like cancer and Alzheimer's.

Advertisement

What Is IDEXX Laboratories' Investment Narrative?

To own IDEXX, you have to believe in the long-term value of its integrated veterinary diagnostics ecosystem, even at a premium valuation and with a high debt load. The latest AI-driven launches in imaging and cancer diagnostics, along with the planned CEO transition to long-time insider Mike Erickson, reinforce the existing thesis rather than rewrite it: IDEXX is leaning harder into connected, software-enabled workflows that can deepen relationships with clinics and support its high-return, recurring-revenue model. In the near term, these products are more likely to be incremental than transformational catalysts, given staged rollouts and the company’s existing scale, but they can strengthen IDEXX’s competitive position and pricing power over time. The bigger swing factor for shareholders remains execution: justifying a high earnings multiple while a relatively new management team takes the reins.

However, investors should be aware of the execution risk that comes with a rich valuation. IDEXX Laboratories' share price has been on the slide but might be up to 37% below fair value. Find out if it's a bargain.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Five fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly US$337 to US$850 per share, showing just how far apart individual investors can be on IDEXX. Set against the company’s high earnings multiple and significant debt position discussed above, this wide spread underlines why it can help to weigh several viewpoints before deciding how IDEXX fits into your own expectations for risk and reward.

data.title: A Look At Intuitive Machines (LUNR) Valuation After The Lanteris Space Systems Acquisition
data.description:
Intuitive Machines (LUNR) has closed its acquisition of Lanteris Space Systems, a move that shifts the company from a pure lunar services focus to a broader prime contractor role in space and defense.

See our latest analysis for Intuitive Machines.

The Lanteris deal comes on the heels of sharp share price swings, with a 59.48% 90 day share price return and a 17.44% 30 day gain, yet a 12.69% 1 year total shareholder return decline that contrasts with a 94.16% 3 year total shareholder return. This points to strong long term momentum but shorter term volatility as investors reassess growth prospects and contract risks.

If this kind of space and defense story has your attention, it could be worth scanning aerospace and defense stocks for other companies capturing similar investor interest.

With LUNR trading at US$19.60 against an average analyst price target of US$17.67, its recent gains and a 94.16% three-year total return raise a key question: is there still upside, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Price to Sales of 10.6x: Is it justified?

With Intuitive Machines closing at US$19.60 and trading on a P/S of 10.6x, the stock sits at a rich premium to both its peers and the wider US Aerospace & Defense group.

The P/S multiple looks at the value the market places on each dollar of revenue, which can be especially relevant for companies that are not yet profitable. For a business like Intuitive Machines, still loss making but with forecast revenue growth of 39.1% per year and expectations of turning profitable within three years, a higher P/S suggests investors are paying up for that growth profile.

What stands out is how far that 10.6x sits above the comparison points. It is materially higher than the US Aerospace & Defense industry average of 3.9x and also above the peer average of 5.8x. This indicates the market is attaching a much richer revenue multiple here than it does for sector and peer companies. The estimated fair P/S ratio of 3.4x is also well below the current level, which points to a valuation that could move closer to that lower benchmark if sentiment or growth expectations cool.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for Intuitive Machines

Result: Price-to-Sales of 10.6x (OVERVALUED)

However, you still have to weigh contract concentration in government work and a US$193.2 million net loss against the premium P/S and recent share price swings.

data.title: Evaluating Apple (AAPL) Valuation After Recent Mixed Share Price Performance
data.description:
Advertisement

Apple’s recent share performance in context

Apple (AAPL) has drawn fresh attention after a mixed stretch in its share performance, with gains over the past year alongside negative returns over the past month and past three months.

See our latest analysis for Apple.

Recent trading shows some loss of momentum, with a 7 day share price return of 4.74% and a 30 day share price return of 8.61% pulling back from a stronger 1 year total shareholder return of 11.14%.

If Apple’s recent moves have you reassessing your tech exposure, it could be a good moment to scan other high growth tech and AI stocks that fit your outlook.

With Apple trading at $247.65, a value score of 1, and mixed short term returns alongside a 1 year total shareholder return of 11.14%, is this weakness a fresh entry point, or is future growth already fully priced in?

Price-to-Earnings of 32.5x: Is it justified?

Apple currently trades on a P/E of 32.5x, which prices its earnings at a premium compared to peers, even after the recent share price pullback.

The P/E ratio compares the share price to earnings per share, so a higher multiple often reflects strong profitability, consistent results, or expectations for future earnings strength.

For Apple, the market appears to be paying up for earnings quality and growth. Earnings grew 19.4% over the past year, ahead of the Tech industry’s 10.9%, and management has converted that into a net profit margin of 26.9%, up from 24% last year. Our fair P/E estimate of 37.2x is materially higher than the current 32.5x. This difference suggests there could be scope for the multiple to move closer to that fair level if current trends in earnings quality and growth persist.

Set against wider peers, though, the premium is clear. Apple’s 32.5x P/E is higher than the peer average of 31.3x and well above the Global Tech industry’s 22.7x, so investors are paying a higher price per dollar of earnings than for many other tech names.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for Apple

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 32.5x (ABOUT RIGHT)

However, the richer 32.5x P/E and recent 30 day and 90 day share price declines mean any disappointment around earnings quality or demand across Apple’s hardware and services could quickly pressure that premium.

Find out about the key risks to this Apple narrative.

Another view: cash flows tell a different story

While the 32.5x P/E looks roughly in line with Apple’s earnings strength, our DCF model points in the opposite direction. On that view, Apple’s current share price of $247.65 sits above an estimated future cash flow value of $225.98, so the stock screens as overvalued on this method.

That gap is not huge, but it does suggest less room for error if growth or margins soften. If one model points to “about right” and another leans to “too rich”, which one do you trust most when real cash is on the line?

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

Simply Wall St performs a discounted cash flow (DCF) on every stock in the world every day (check out Apple for example). We show the entire calculation in full. You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 881 undervalued stocks based on their cash flows. If you save a screener we even alert you when new companies match - so you never miss a potential opportunity.

data.title: Check Point Launches AI Exposure Management As Shares Face Recent Pullback
data.description:
• Check Point Software Technologies (NasdaqGS:CHKP) has introduced Check Point Exposure Management, an AI-driven cybersecurity product focused on automated and AI-based attacks.
  • The solution is designed to manage and remediate cyber risk across complex environments by integrating with more than 75 security controls.
  • The launch expands Check Point's presence in continuous, intelligence-driven threat exposure management at a time of rising AI-related cyber threats.

For investors watching NasdaqGS:CHKP, this product arrives as the shares trade at around $174.36. The stock has seen a 35.8% return over 3 years and 30.9% over 5 years, alongside more recent declines of 7.3% over the past week, 8.6% over the past month, 3.7% year to date, and 10.3% over the past year.

This launch may influence how you think about Check Point's role in AI-focused cybersecurity, particularly as enterprises look for ways to consolidate tools and respond faster to complex threats. The immediate questions for investors are likely to center on customer adoption, how the product fits into existing security budgets, and whether it affects Check Point's competitive position in exposure management.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Check Point Software Technologies by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Check Point Software Technologies.

How Check Point Software Technologies stacks up against its biggest competitors

Check Point Exposure Management speaks directly to a key pain point for large enterprises, which often juggle many security tools without a clear view of which vulnerabilities matter most. By tying together threat intelligence, dark web insight, attack surface mapping, and automated remediation across more than 75 security controls, this offering pushes Check Point deeper into continuous threat exposure management, a segment that many security buyers are already evaluating.

Advertisement

Risks and rewards for Check Point Software Technologies

  • The new product could strengthen Check Point's role with existing customers who want to get more value out of tools they already own, rather than adding yet another standalone platform.
  • Aligning with Gartner's CTEM framework and covering roughly 90% of major security vendors positions Exposure Management as a potential entry point into broader Check Point offerings over time.
  • The market for exposure and attack surface management is already crowded, so investor expectations around share gains may hinge on how quickly enterprises adopt automated remediation at scale.
  • Security buyers can be cautious about automated changes to critical controls, which may slow full use of the platform's remediation features even when it is technically deployed.

What to watch next

From here, the key things to watch are early customer references, how often Exposure Management is sold into large existing accounts versus new logos, and any commentary from management about how it is bundled or priced relative to the rest of the portfolio. If you want to keep following how this story develops alongside other investor views, you can check out what the wider community is saying.

data.title: Joby Aviation (JOBY) Is Down 5.4% After Dubai Progress Meets Cooler Analyst Views Has The Bull Case Changed?
data.description:
• In recent weeks, Joby Aviation has continued to advance its electric air taxi ambitions, combining early revenue from its Blade acquisition, progress toward FAA certification, manufacturing expansion with Toyota, and preparations for commercial services in Dubai.
  • At the same time, analysts have cooled their outlook, arguing that much of this operational progress is already reflected in investor expectations, prompting a reassessment of Joby’s near-term potential.
  • We’ll now examine how this mix of growing Dubai-focused operational readiness and more cautious analyst sentiment shapes Joby Aviation’s investment narrative.

Uncover the next big thing with financially sound penny stocks that balance risk and reward.

Advertisement

What Is Joby Aviation's Investment Narrative?

For Joby Aviation, you really have to believe that early operational steps like Dubai preparations, the Uber integration of Blade services, and progress toward FAA certification can eventually justify today’s rich valuation despite heavy losses and no near-term profitability forecast. The latest news reinforces that story on both sides: operationally, Joby is ticking boxes with simulators, vertiport partnerships, and real Blade revenue, yet the stock’s pullback and cooler analyst tone suggest that these milestones may already be baked into expectations. Near term, the key catalysts still look like certification progress, visible momentum toward Dubai service, and evidence that manufacturing can ramp without major hiccups. On the risk side, cash burn, a high price to book multiple, and the possibility of future dilution remain front and center, even with the recent good news.

However, one risk around cash needs and potential dilution is something investors should not overlook. The analysis detailed in our Joby Aviation valuation report hints at an inflated share price compared to its estimated value.

Exploring Other Perspectives

When you set that against concerns about high cash burn and a rich valuation multiple, it becomes clear that understanding these contrasting viewpoints may be crucial to judging how the company’s execution could influence longer term outcomes.

data.title: Returns Are Gaining Momentum At Synectics (LON:SNX)
data.description:
To find a multi-bagger stock, what are the underlying trends we should look for in a business? Firstly, we'll want to see a proven return on capital employed (ROCE) that is increasing, and secondly, an expanding base of capital employed. Basically this means that a company has profitable initiatives that it can continue to reinvest in, which is a trait of a compounding machine. So on that note, Synectics (LON:SNX) looks quite promising in regards to its trends of return on capital.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

What Is Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)?

For those that aren't sure what ROCE is, it measures the amount of pre-tax profits a company can generate from the capital employed in its business. Analysts use this formula to calculate it for Synectics:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.12 = UK£5.6m ÷ (UK£65m - UK£18m) (Based on the trailing twelve months to May 2025).

Thus, Synectics has an ROCE of 12%. That's a pretty standard return and it's in line with the industry average of 12%.

View our latest analysis for Synectics

Historical performance is a great place to start when researching a stock so above you can see the gauge for Synectics' ROCE against it's prior returns. If you'd like to look at how Synectics has performed in the past in other metrics, you can view this free graph of Synectics' past earnings, revenue and cash flow.

The Trend Of ROCE

We're delighted to see that Synectics is reaping rewards from its investments and has now broken into profitability. The company was generating losses five years ago, but has managed to turn it around and as we saw earlier is now earning 12%, which is always encouraging. On top of that, what's interesting is that the amount of capital being employed has remained steady, so the business hasn't needed to put any additional money to work to generate these higher returns. With no noticeable increase in capital employed, it's worth knowing what the company plans on doing going forward in regards to reinvesting and growing the business. Because in the end, a business can only get so efficient.

The Bottom Line

In summary, we're delighted to see that Synectics has been able to increase efficiencies and earn higher rates of return on the same amount of capital. And a remarkable 115% total return over the last five years tells us that investors are expecting more good things to come in the future. So given the stock has proven it has promising trends, it's worth researching the company further to see if these trends are likely to persist.

If you'd like to know about the risks facing Synectics, we've discovered 3 warning signs that you should be aware of.

While Synectics may not currently earn the highest returns, we've compiled a list of companies that currently earn more than 25% return on equity. Check out this free list here.

data.title: Assessing Wärtsilä (HLSE:WRT1V) Valuation After Strong 1 Year Return And Elevated P/E Multiple
data.description:
Advertisement

Recent performance snapshot

With no single headline event driving attention today, Wärtsilä Oyj Abp (HLSE:WRT1V) has still drawn interest after a period of positive share returns and identifiable reported revenue and net income figures.

Over the past month, the stock shows a 9.17% return, with around 24.22% in the past 3 months and 6.11% year to date. The reported 1 year total return stands at 86.08%.

See our latest analysis for Wärtsilä Oyj Abp.

At a share price of €32.98, the recent 9.17% 1 month share price return sits on top of a strong 1 year total shareholder return of 86.08% and a very large 5 year total shareholder return. Together, these figures indicate that momentum has been building over time.

If Wärtsilä's performance has you looking wider, this could be a good moment to broaden your search and check out aerospace and defense stocks as another source of ideas.

So, with a €32.98 share price, an 86.08% 1 year total return, and reported revenue of €6,768.0m alongside net income of €600.0m, is Wärtsilä still offering upside, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Price-to-Earnings of 32.3x: Is it justified?

At a €32.98 share price and a P/E of 32.3x, Wärtsilä is priced well above both its Machinery peers and the levels our models flag as comfortable.

The P/E multiple compares the current share price with earnings per share and is a quick way to see how much you are paying for each € of profit. For an established capital goods business, a higher than average P/E usually means the market is paying up for its earnings profile.

Here, the stock is described as expensive on several fronts. Its 32.3x P/E sits above the European Machinery industry average of 21.4x and also above the peer average of 22.8x, which suggests the market is pricing in stronger earnings than those peers. In addition, the estimated fair P/E of 25.2x implies a level that the valuation could trend toward if expectations cool from where they are today.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for Wärtsilä Oyj Abp

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 32.3x (OVERVALUED)

However, you also have to weigh the risk that expectations embedded in a 32.3x P/E ease off, or that Wärtsilä’s €6,768.0m revenue and €600.0m net income disappoint investors.

Find out about the key risks to this Wärtsilä Oyj Abp narrative.

Another view on value

Our DCF model also suggests Wärtsilä is not cheap, with the current €32.98 share price sitting above an estimated future cash flow value of €28.97. This provides both earnings-based and cash flow-based perspectives that point to a rich price. Which signal matters more to you?

data.title: How New 2026 Production Guidance and Leadership Shift At Parex Resources (TSX:PXT) Has Changed Its Investment Story
data.description:
• Parex Resources Inc. recently published its 2026 operational guidance, outlining average production of 45,000–49,000 boe/d and a fully funded US$300 million capital program, while also confirming Board Chair Wayne Foo will retire in May 2026 with Vice Chair Glenn McNamara stepping into the role.
  • This combination of refreshed production plans, capital allocation priorities and planned leadership transition offers a clearer view of how Parex intends to balance investment, risk management and corporate continuity over the coming year.
  • We’ll now examine how Parex’s updated 2026 production guidance and capital program shape the company’s investment narrative and risk profile.

Find companies with promising cash flow potential yet trading below their fair value.

Advertisement

What Is Parex Resources' Investment Narrative?

To own Parex today, you need to be comfortable with a story built around disciplined Colombian oil production, active capital returns and measured exploration rather than rapid expansion. The new 2026 guidance, with 45,000–49,000 boe/d and a fully funded US$300 million capital program, largely validates the existing near term catalysts: execution on Putumayo and Foothills drilling, and how management balances that spend against dividends and buybacks, including the newly approved normal course issuer bid. The scheduled Board transition from Wayne Foo to Glenn McNamara looks orderly, so governance risk does not materially change, but it puts a bit more focus on whether capital allocation stays as shareholder friendly as it has been. For now, the bigger swing factors remain commodity prices, project delivery and the sustainability of that near 8% dividend.

However, the generous dividend and buybacks come with coverage and reinvestment trade offs investors should understand. Parex Resources' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

data.title: Voestalpine's (VIE:VOE) investors will be pleased with their splendid 124% return over the last year
data.description:
The most you can lose on any stock (assuming you don't use leverage) is 100% of your money. But when you pick a company that is really flourishing, you can make more than 100%. Take, for example Voestalpine AG (VIE:VOE). Its share price is already up an impressive 119% in the last twelve months. On top of that, the share price is up 26% in about a quarter. But this move may well have been assisted by the reasonably buoyant market (up 12% in 90 days). Looking back further, the stock price is 32% higher than it was three years ago.

So let's assess the underlying fundamentals over the last 1 year and see if they've moved in lock-step with shareholder returns.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

While the efficient markets hypothesis continues to be taught by some, it has been proven that markets are over-reactive dynamic systems, and investors are not always rational. By comparing earnings per share (EPS) and share price changes over time, we can get a feel for how investor attitudes to a company have morphed over time.

During the last year Voestalpine grew its earnings per share, moving from a loss to a profit.

When a company is just on the edge of profitability it can be well worth considering other metrics in order to more precisely gauge growth (and therefore understand share price movements).

We are skeptical of the suggestion that the 1.5% dividend yield would entice buyers to the stock. Unfortunately Voestalpine's fell 5.7% over twelve months. So using a snapshot of key business metrics doesn't give us a good picture of why the market is bidding up the stock.

You can see how earnings and revenue have changed over time in the image below (click on the chart to see the exact values).

Voestalpine is a well known stock, with plenty of analyst coverage, suggesting some visibility into future growth. Given we have quite a good number of analyst forecasts, it might be well worth checking out this free chart depicting consensus estimates.

Advertisement

What About Dividends?

It is important to consider the total shareholder return, as well as the share price return, for any given stock. The TSR is a return calculation that accounts for the value of cash dividends (assuming that any dividend received was reinvested) and the calculated value of any discounted capital raisings and spin-offs. Arguably, the TSR gives a more comprehensive picture of the return generated by a stock. We note that for Voestalpine the TSR over the last 1 year was 124%, which is better than the share price return mentioned above. The dividends paid by the company have thusly boosted the total shareholder return.

A Different Perspective

We're pleased to report that Voestalpine shareholders have received a total shareholder return of 124% over one year. And that does include the dividend. That's better than the annualised return of 9% over half a decade, implying that the company is doing better recently. In the best case scenario, this may hint at some real business momentum, implying that now could be a great time to delve deeper. Is Voestalpine cheap compared to other companies? These 3 valuation measures might help you decide.

We will like Voestalpine better if we see some big insider buys. While we wait, check out this free list of undervalued stocks (mostly small caps) with considerable, recent, insider buying.

Please note, the market returns quoted in this article reflect the market weighted average returns of stocks that currently trade on Austrian exchanges.

data.title: Nongfu Spring's (HKG:9633) investors will be pleased with their favorable 49% return over the last year
data.description:
These days it's easy to simply buy an index fund, and your returns should (roughly) match the market. But one can do better than that by picking better than average stocks (as part of a diversified portfolio). For example, the Nongfu Spring Co., Ltd. (HKG:9633) share price is up 46% in the last 1 year, clearly besting the market return of around 33% (not including dividends). If it can keep that out-performance up over the long term, investors will do very well! The longer term returns have not been as good, with the stock price only 17% higher than it was three years ago.

Let's take a look at the underlying fundamentals over the longer term, and see if they've been consistent with shareholders returns.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

To paraphrase Benjamin Graham: Over the short term the market is a voting machine, but over the long term it's a weighing machine. One imperfect but simple way to consider how the market perception of a company has shifted is to compare the change in the earnings per share (EPS) with the share price movement.

During the last year Nongfu Spring grew its earnings per share (EPS) by 7.7%. This EPS growth is significantly lower than the 46% increase in the share price. So it's fair to assume the market has a higher opinion of the business than it a year ago.

You can see below how EPS has changed over time (discover the exact values by clicking on the image).

This free interactive report on Nongfu Spring's earnings, revenue and cash flow is a great place to start, if you want to investigate the stock further.

Advertisement

What About Dividends?

When looking at investment returns, it is important to consider the difference between total shareholder return (TSR) and share price return. Whereas the share price return only reflects the change in the share price, the TSR includes the value of dividends (assuming they were reinvested) and the benefit of any discounted capital raising or spin-off. It's fair to say that the TSR gives a more complete picture for stocks that pay a dividend. As it happens, Nongfu Spring's TSR for the last 1 year was 49%, which exceeds the share price return mentioned earlier. The dividends paid by the company have thusly boosted the total shareholder return.

A Different Perspective

It's good to see that Nongfu Spring has rewarded shareholders with a total shareholder return of 49% in the last twelve months. And that does include the dividend. That certainly beats the loss of about 2% per year over the last half decade. The long term loss makes us cautious, but the short term TSR gain certainly hints at a brighter future. I find it very interesting to look at share price over the long term as a proxy for business performance. But to truly gain insight, we need to consider other information, too. Take risks, for example - Nongfu Spring has 1 warning sign we think you should be aware of.

For those who like to find winning investments this free list of undervalued companies with recent insider purchasing, could be just the ticket.

Please note, the market returns quoted in this article reflect the market weighted average returns of stocks that currently trade on Hong Kong exchanges.

data.title: Despite shrinking by €168m in the past week, CIE Automotive (BME:CIE) shareholders are still up 55% over 5 years
data.description:
The main point of investing for the long term is to make money. But more than that, you probably want to see it rise more than the market average. Unfortunately for shareholders, while the CIE Automotive, S.A. (BME:CIE) share price is up 37% in the last five years, that's less than the market return. Looking at the last year alone, the stock is up 13%.

Since the long term performance has been good but there's been a recent pullback of 4.7%, let's check if the fundamentals match the share price.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

While the efficient markets hypothesis continues to be taught by some, it has been proven that markets are over-reactive dynamic systems, and investors are not always rational. By comparing earnings per share (EPS) and share price changes over time, we can get a feel for how investor attitudes to a company have morphed over time.

During the five years of share price growth, CIE Automotive moved from a loss to profitability. That's generally thought to be a genuine positive, so investors may expect to see an increasing share price.

You can see how EPS has changed over time in the image below (click on the chart to see the exact values).

Before buying or selling a stock, we always recommend a close examination of historic growth trends, available here.

Advertisement

What About Dividends?

As well as measuring the share price return, investors should also consider the total shareholder return (TSR). Whereas the share price return only reflects the change in the share price, the TSR includes the value of dividends (assuming they were reinvested) and the benefit of any discounted capital raising or spin-off. So for companies that pay a generous dividend, the TSR is often a lot higher than the share price return. We note that for CIE Automotive the TSR over the last 5 years was 55%, which is better than the share price return mentioned above. This is largely a result of its dividend payments!

A Different Perspective

CIE Automotive shareholders gained a total return of 17% during the year. But that was short of the market average. On the bright side, that's still a gain, and it's actually better than the average return of 9% over half a decade It is possible that returns will improve along with the business fundamentals. It's always interesting to track share price performance over the longer term. But to understand CIE Automotive better, we need to consider many other factors. data.title: Coromandel International (NSE:COROMANDEL) Could Be Struggling To Allocate Capital
data.description:
If we want to find a stock that could multiply over the long term, what are the underlying trends we should look for? Typically, we'll want to notice a trend of growing return on capital employed (ROCE) and alongside that, an expanding base of capital employed. Put simply, these types of businesses are compounding machines, meaning they are continually reinvesting their earnings at ever-higher rates of return. So while Coromandel International (NSE:COROMANDEL) has a high ROCE right now, lets see what we can decipher from how returns are changing.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

Understanding Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)

For those who don't know, ROCE is a measure of a company's yearly pre-tax profit (its return), relative to the capital employed in the business. Analysts use this formula to calculate it for Coromandel International:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.20 = ₹28b ÷ (₹247b - ₹111b) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

Therefore, Coromandel International has an ROCE of 20%. In absolute terms that's a great return and it's even better than the Chemicals industry average of 12%.

Check out our latest analysis for Coromandel International

Above you can see how the current ROCE for Coromandel International compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you're interested, you can view the analysts predictions in our free analyst report for Coromandel International .

What The Trend Of ROCE Can Tell Us

On the surface, the trend of ROCE at Coromandel International doesn't inspire confidence. Historically returns on capital were even higher at 38%, but they have dropped over the last five years. However, given capital employed and revenue have both increased it appears that the business is currently pursuing growth, at the consequence of short term returns. And if the increased capital generates additional returns, the business, and thus shareholders, will benefit in the long run.

Another thing to note, Coromandel International has a high ratio of current liabilities to total assets of 45%. This can bring about some risks because the company is basically operating with a rather large reliance on its suppliers or other sorts of short-term creditors. Ideally we'd like to see this reduce as that would mean fewer obligations bearing risks.

The Bottom Line

While returns have fallen for Coromandel International in recent times, we're encouraged to see that sales are growing and that the business is reinvesting in its operations. And the stock has done incredibly well with a 187% return over the last five years, so long term investors are no doubt ecstatic with that result. So while the underlying trends could already be accounted for by investors, we still think this stock is worth looking into further.

If you'd like to know about the risks facing Coromandel International, we've discovered 1 warning sign that you should be aware of.

If you'd like to see other companies earning high returns, check out our free list of companies earning high returns with solid balance sheets here.

data.title: C-Lab Ltd's (TLV:CLAB) 27% Price Boost Is Out Of Tune With Revenues
data.description:
C-Lab Ltd (TLV:CLAB) shares have continued their recent momentum with a 27% gain in the last month alone. But the gains over the last month weren't enough to make shareholders whole, as the share price is still down 7.4% in the last twelve months.

Even after such a large jump in price, it's still not a stretch to say that C-Lab's price-to-sales (or "P/S") ratio of 1.3x right now seems quite "middle-of-the-road" compared to the Tech industry in Israel, where the median P/S ratio is around 1.4x. Although, it's not wise to simply ignore the P/S without explanation as investors may be disregarding a distinct opportunity or a costly mistake.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

See our latest analysis for C-Lab

Advertisement

How C-Lab Has Been Performing

As an illustration, revenue has deteriorated at C-Lab over the last year, which is not ideal at all. It might be that many expect the company to put the disappointing revenue performance behind them over the coming period, which has kept the P/S from falling. If not, then existing shareholders may be a little nervous about the viability of the share price.

Want the full picture on earnings, revenue and cash flow for the company? Then our free report on C-Lab will help you shine a light on its historical performance.

How Is C-Lab's Revenue Growth Trending?

The only time you'd be comfortable seeing a P/S like C-Lab's is when the company's growth is tracking the industry closely.

Taking a look back first, the company's revenue growth last year wasn't something to get excited about as it posted a disappointing decline of 34%. This means it has also seen a slide in revenue over the longer-term as revenue is down 23% in total over the last three years. Therefore, it's fair to say the revenue growth recently has been undesirable for the company.

In contrast to the company, the rest of the industry is expected to grow by 30% over the next year, which really puts the company's recent medium-term revenue decline into perspective.

With this in mind, we find it worrying that C-Lab's P/S exceeds that of its industry peers. It seems most investors are ignoring the recent poor growth rate and are hoping for a turnaround in the company's business prospects. Only the boldest would assume these prices are sustainable as a continuation of recent revenue trends is likely to weigh on the share price eventually.

What We Can Learn From C-Lab's P/S?

C-Lab's stock has a lot of momentum behind it lately, which has brought its P/S level with the rest of the industry. We'd say the price-to-sales ratio's power isn't primarily as a valuation instrument but rather to gauge current investor sentiment and future expectations.

We find it unexpected that C-Lab trades at a P/S ratio that is comparable to the rest of the industry, despite experiencing declining revenues during the medium-term, while the industry as a whole is expected to grow. When we see revenue heading backwards in the context of growing industry forecasts, it'd make sense to expect a possible share price decline on the horizon, sending the moderate P/S lower. Unless the the circumstances surrounding the recent medium-term improve, it wouldn't be wrong to expect a a difficult period ahead for the company's shareholders.

And what about other risks? Every company has them, and we've spotted 2 warning signs for C-Lab you should know about.

If strong companies turning a profit tickle your fancy, then you'll want to check out this free list of interesting companies that trade on a low P/E (but have proven they can grow earnings).

data.title: Further Upside For Atende S.A. (WSE:ATD) Shares Could Introduce Price Risks After 28% Bounce
data.description:
Those holding Atende S.A. (WSE:ATD) shares would be relieved that the share price has rebounded 28% in the last thirty days, but it needs to keep going to repair the recent damage it has caused to investor portfolios. The last 30 days bring the annual gain to a very sharp 27%.

Even after such a large jump in price, Atende may still be sending bullish signals at the moment with its price-to-sales (or "P/S") ratio of 0.4x, since almost half of all companies in the IT industry in Poland have P/S ratios greater than 1x and even P/S higher than 3x are not unusual. Nonetheless, we'd need to dig a little deeper to determine if there is a rational basis for the reduced P/S.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

See our latest analysis for Atende

Advertisement

How Has Atende Performed Recently?

The recent revenue growth at Atende would have to be considered satisfactory if not spectacular. One possibility is that the P/S ratio is low because investors think this good revenue growth might actually underperform the broader industry in the near future. If that doesn't eventuate, then existing shareholders may have reason to be optimistic about the future direction of the share price.

Although there are no analyst estimates available for Atende, take a look at this free data-rich visualisation to see how the company stacks up on earnings, revenue and cash flow.

What Are Revenue Growth Metrics Telling Us About The Low P/S?

The only time you'd be truly comfortable seeing a P/S as low as Atende's is when the company's growth is on track to lag the industry.

Taking a look back first, we see that the company managed to grow revenues by a handy 3.9% last year. This was backed up an excellent period prior to see revenue up by 53% in total over the last three years. Accordingly, shareholders would have definitely welcomed those medium-term rates of revenue growth.

This is in contrast to the rest of the industry, which is expected to grow by 3.9% over the next year, materially lower than the company's recent medium-term annualised growth rates.

In light of this, it's peculiar that Atende's P/S sits below the majority of other companies. Apparently some shareholders believe the recent performance has exceeded its limits and have been accepting significantly lower selling prices.

What Does Atende's P/S Mean For Investors?

Atende's stock price has surged recently, but its but its P/S still remains modest. Generally, our preference is to limit the use of the price-to-sales ratio to establishing what the market thinks about the overall health of a company.

Our examination of Atende revealed its three-year revenue trends aren't boosting its P/S anywhere near as much as we would have predicted, given they look better than current industry expectations. When we see strong revenue with faster-than-industry growth, we assume there are some significant underlying risks to the company's ability to make money which is applying downwards pressure on the P/S ratio. At least price risks look to be very low if recent medium-term revenue trends continue, but investors seem to think future revenue could see a lot of volatility.

You need to take note of risks, for example - Atende has 3 warning signs (and 2 which are a bit unpleasant) we think you should know about.

It's important to make sure you look for a great company, not just the first idea you come across. So if growing profitability aligns with your idea of a great company, take a peek at this free list of interesting companies with strong recent earnings growth (and a low P/E).

data.title: Rohto PharmaceuticalLtd (TSE:4527) shareholders have earned a 12% CAGR over the last five years
data.description:
If you buy and hold a stock for many years, you'd hope to be making a profit. But more than that, you probably want to see it rise more than the market average. Unfortunately for shareholders, while the Rohto Pharmaceutical Co.,Ltd. (TSE:4527) share price is up 69% in the last five years, that's less than the market return. Zooming in, the stock is actually down 3.1% in the last year.

Now it's worth having a look at the company's fundamentals too, because that will help us determine if the long term shareholder return has matched the performance of the underlying business.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

To quote Buffett, 'Ships will sail around the world but the Flat Earth Society will flourish. There will continue to be wide discrepancies between price and value in the marketplace...' By comparing earnings per share (EPS) and share price changes over time, we can get a feel for how investor attitudes to a company have morphed over time.

During five years of share price growth, Rohto PharmaceuticalLtd achieved compound earnings per share (EPS) growth of 20% per year. This EPS growth is higher than the 11% average annual increase in the share price. Therefore, it seems the market has become relatively pessimistic about the company.

You can see how EPS has changed over time in the image below (click on the chart to see the exact values).

We know that Rohto PharmaceuticalLtd has improved its bottom line lately, but is it going to grow revenue? Check if analysts think Rohto PharmaceuticalLtd will grow revenue in the future.

Advertisement

What About Dividends?

When looking at investment returns, it is important to consider the difference between total shareholder return (TSR) and share price return. The TSR incorporates the value of any spin-offs or discounted capital raisings, along with any dividends, based on the assumption that the dividends are reinvested. So for companies that pay a generous dividend, the TSR is often a lot higher than the share price return. In the case of Rohto PharmaceuticalLtd, it has a TSR of 78% for the last 5 years. That exceeds its share price return that we previously mentioned. This is largely a result of its dividend payments!

A Different Perspective

While the broader market gained around 34% in the last year, Rohto PharmaceuticalLtd shareholders lost 1.4% (even including dividends). However, keep in mind that even the best stocks will sometimes underperform the market over a twelve month period. Longer term investors wouldn't be so upset, since they would have made 12%, each year, over five years. If the fundamental data continues to indicate long term sustainable growth, the current sell-off could be an opportunity worth considering. Is Rohto PharmaceuticalLtd cheap compared to other companies? These 3 valuation measures might help you decide.

We will like Rohto PharmaceuticalLtd better if we see some big insider buys. While we wait, check out this free list of undervalued stocks (mostly small caps) with considerable, recent, insider buying.

Please note, the market returns quoted in this article reflect the market weighted average returns of stocks that currently trade on Japanese exchanges.
